join_order.notes: Use customer_segment_postings to enumerate BUILDING customer rowids, load c_custkey, and probe orders_cust_postings directly.

join_order.build: orders(filtered by customer join and o_orderdate < 1995-03-15)
join_order.notes: For each surviving order, immediately traverse lineitem_order_postings, apply l_shipdate > 1995-03-15, and emit one aggregated order result without materializing a global qualifying-orders array.

scan.table: customer(filtered via postings) -> orders(postings expansion) -> lineitem(postings expansion)

data_structures.aggregation: register_accumulator(per-order revenue in fused loop) + fixed_min_heap(10) per thread

indexes.purpose: resolve BUILDING customers without scanning customer table
expand filtered customer keys to candidate orders
expand qualifying orders to matching lineitems only

optimization_notes: Root cause: Category A plan mismatch. The current implementation spends most time in build_joins materializing and copying a 1.46M-row qualifying-orders intermediate, then rereads it in main_scan to reach lineitems. Replace that two-phase flow with a fused index-nested-loop pipeline: enumerate BUILDING customers from postings, expand orders through orders_cust_postings, apply o_orderdate immediately, traverse lineitem_order_postings for each surviving order, apply l_shipdate, accumulate revenue in registers, and push positive results straight into thread-local top-k state before a final merge.
